title: Fix: Safe grave number change, consistent alley sorting, prevent data loss

requirements.text: Fix the grave number change logic in the backend so that updating a grave's plot number never deletes or detaches the parent alley. The operation must be atomic: copy all grave data (including all deceased persons, owner, status, payment info) to the new key, then remove the old key, while keeping the alley record fully intact and all its grave references updated correctly.

quotes: po zmianie numeru grobu aplikacja usuwa całą aleje wcześniej istniejącą
Wprowadził błąd w logice zmiany numeru grobu, który usunął dane alei

acceptanceCriteria: Changing a grave's plot number does not remove or empty the parent alley
All deceased persons, owner data, status and payment date are preserved after a plot number change
The alley retains all its other graves after a plot number change on one grave
If the new plot number already exists, the operation is rejected with an error message rather than overwriting

requirements.text: Ensure alleys are always stored and retrieved in a consistent, predictable sort order. Alleys must be sorted alphabetically by name (or numerically if names are numeric) in all backend query responses. This sort must be applied to every function that returns alleys or cemetery layout data.

quotes: aplikacja umieściła ją na końcu wszystkich alei, czyli nie zachowując żadnej spójności chronologicznej, alfabetycznej, numerycznej - totalny chaos
Stałe sortowanie alei (alfabetyczne lub numeryczne) — zawsze, wszędzie

acceptanceCriteria: All backend endpoints that return alley lists return them sorted alphabetically (or numerically for numeric names)
A newly added or restored alley appears in its correct sorted position, not appended at the end
Sort order is stable across repeated calls

requirements.text: Apply consistent alley sorting on the frontend in all components that render alleys (AdminGraveTileMap, CemeteryLayoutManager, GraveTileMap, GraveResultsList, and any other alley lists). Sort alleys alphabetically/numerically on the client side as a safety fallback, so even if backend order changes the UI always shows a predictable order.

quotes: aplikacja umieściła ją na końcu wszystkich alei, czyli nie zachowując żadnej spójności chronologicznej, alfabetycznej, numerycznej - totalny chaos

acceptanceCriteria: AdminGraveTileMap renders alleys in alphabetical/numerical order
GraveTileMap renders alleys in alphabetical/numerical order
CemeteryLayoutManager displays alleys in alphabetical/numerical order
Restoring or adding an alley immediately places it in the correct sorted position in all views without requiring a page reload

requirements.text: Add a safeguard in GraveEditDialog (and any other edit dialogs) that warns the admin before saving a plot number change, clearly stating that this operation will change the grave identifier. Show a confirmation step specifically for plot number changes distinct from the general save action, to prevent accidental data-loss operations.

quotes: Usunięcie możliwości przypadkowego uszkodzenia danych przy edycji

acceptanceCriteria: When the admin changes the plot number field and clicks Save, a confirmation dialog appears before the save is executed
The confirmation message explicitly mentions that the plot/grave number is being changed
Cancelling the confirmation leaves all data unchanged
Confirming proceeds with the safe atomic update from REQ-1

constraints: Do not alter any other data fields or schema beyond what is required to fix the grave number change and alley sorting logic
Do not remove the plot number editing feature — only make it safe
Do not touch frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Recovering previously lost cemetery data — this is a code fix only
Adding new features beyond the three bug fixes described above
Changing the visual design or theme of the application
